topic: Fish gill anatomy (filaments + whole fish + lamellae)
image_prompt: SVG: three-panel fish gill diagram — (left) three stacked gill arches packed with red filaments with a blood-vessel arrow labelled 'blood vessels' and 'oxygen-poor blood' / 'oxygen-rich blood'; (right) side view of a green fish with mouth open, gill cavity exposed showing red gill arches, eye, scales, fins and arrows labelled 'water flows in through the mouth' / 'water flows out through the gills'; (bottom centre) close-up of two stacked ridged lamellae with thin capillary lines labelled 'the gills are made up of thin sheets called lamellae'.
